author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XCI

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This Psalm is that from which the Devil dared to tempt our Lord Jesus Christ: let us therefore attend to it, that thus armed, we may be enabled to resist the tempter, not presuming in ourselves, but in Him who before us was tempted, that we might not be overcome when tempted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Temptation to Him was not necessary: the temptation of Christ is our learning, but if we listen to His answers to the devil, in order that, when ourselves are tempted, we may answer in like manner, we are then entering through the gate, as ye have heard it read in the Gospel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what is to enter by the gate?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To enter by Christ, who Himself said, “I am the door:” and to enter through Christ, is to imitate His ways.…He urges us to imitate Him in those works which He could not have done had He not been made Man; for how could He endure sufferings, unless He had become a Man?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How could He otherwise have died, been crucified, been humbled?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus then do thou, when thou sufferest the troubles of this world, which the devil, openly by men, or secretly, as in Job’s case, inflicts; be courageous, be of long suffering; “thou shalt dwell under the defence of the Most High,” as this Psalm expresses it: for if thou depart from the help of the Most High, without strength to aid thyself, thou wilt fall.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For many men are brave, when they are enduring persecution from men, and see them openly rage against themselves: imagining they are then imitating the sufferings of Christ, in case men openly persecute them; but if assailed by the hidden attack of the devil, they believe they are not being crowned by Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Never fear when thou dost imitate Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when the devil tempted our Lord, there was no man in the wilderness; he tempted Him secretly; but he was conquered, and conquered too when openly attacking Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This do thou, if thou wishest to enter by the door, when the enemy secretly assails thee, when he asks for a man that he may do him some hurt by bodily troubles, by fever, by sickness, or any other bodily sufferings, like those of Job.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saw not the devil, yet he acknowledged the power of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He knew that the devil had no power against him, unless from the Almighty Ruler of all things he received that power: the whole glory he gave to God, power to the devil he gave not.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He then who so imitates Christ as to endure all the troubles of this world, with his hopes set upon God, that he falls into no snare, is broken down by no panic fears, he it is “who dwelleth under the defence of the Most High, who shall abide under the protection of God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 1), in the words with which the Psalm, which you have heard and sung, begins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: You will recognise the words, so well known, in which the devil tempted our Lord, when we come to them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He shall say unto the Lord, Thou art my taker up, and my refuge: my God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who speaks thus to the Lord?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He who dwelleth under the defence of the Most High:” not under his own defence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He dwelleth under the defence of the Most High, who is not proud, like those who ate, that they might become as Gods, and lost the immortality in which they were made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they chose to dwell under a defence of their own, not under that of the Most High: thus they listened to the suggestions of the serpent, and despised the precept of God: and discovered at last that what God threatened, not what the devil promised, had come to pass in them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus then do thou say also, “In Him will I trust.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He Himself shall deliver me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 3), not I myself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe whether he teaches anything but this, that all our trust be in God, none in man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence shall he deliver thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “From the snare of the hunter, and from a harsh word.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Deliverance from the hunter’s net is indeed a great blessing: but how is deliverance from a harsh word so?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Many have fallen into the hunter’s net through a harsh word.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is it that I say?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The devil and his angels spread their snares, as hunters do: and those who walk in Christ tread afar from those snares: for he dares not spread his net in Christ: he sets it on the verge of the way, not in the way.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let then thy way be Christ, and thou shalt not fall into the snares of the devil.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what is, “from a harsh word”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The devil has entrapped many by a harsh word: for instance, those who profess Christianity among Pagans suffer insult from the heathen: they blush when they hear reproach, and shrinking out of their path in consequence, fall into the hunter’s snares.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And yet what will a harsh word do to you?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Can the snares with which the enemy entraps you by means of reproaches, do nothing to you?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nets are usually spread for birds at the end of a hedge, and stones are thrown into the hedge: those stones will not harm the birds.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When did any one ever hit a bird by throwing a stone into a hedge?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the bird, frightened at the harmless noise, falls into the nets; and thus men who fear the vain reproaches of their calumniators, and who blush at unprovoked insults, fall into the snares of the hunters, and are taken captive by the devil…Just as among the heathen, the Christian who fears their reproaches falls into the snare of the hunter: so among the Christians, those who endeavour to be more diligent and better than the rest, are doomed to bear insults from Christians themselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then doth it profit, my brother, if thou occasionally find a city in which there is no heathen?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No one there insults a man because he is a Christian, for this reason, that there is no Pagan therein: but there are many Christians who lead a bad life, among whom those who are resolved to live righteously, and to be sober among the drunken, and chaste among the unchaste, and amid the consulters of astrologers sincerely to worship God, and to ask after no such things, and among spectators of frivolous shows will go only to church, suffer from those very Christians reproaches, and harsh words, when they address such a one, “Thou art the mighty, the righteous, thou art Elias, thou art Peter: thou hast come from heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They insult him: whichever way he turns, he hears harsh sayings on each side: and if he fears, and abandons the way of Christ, he falls into the snares of the hunters.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what is it, when he hears such words, not to swerve from the way?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: On hearing them, what comfort has he, which prevents his heeding them, and enables him to enter by the door?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let him say; What words am I called, who am a servant and a sinner?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To my Lord Jesus they said, “Thou hast a devil.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: You have just heard the harsh words spoken against our Lord: it was not necessary for our Lord to suffer this, but in doing so He has warned thee against harsh words, lest thou fall into the snares of the hunters.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He shall defend thee between His shoulders, and thou shalt hope under His wings” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He says this, that thy protection may not be to thee from thyself, that thou mayest not imagine that thou canst defend thyself; He will defend thee, to deliver thee from the hunter’s snare, and from an harsh word.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The expression, “between His shoulders,” may be understood both in front and behind: for the shoulders are about the head; but in the words, “thou shalt hope under His wings,” it is clear that the protection of the wings of God expanded places thee between His shoulders, so that God’s wings on this side and that have thee in the midst, where thou shalt not fear lest any one hurt thee: only be thou careful never to leave that spot, where no foe dares approach.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If the hen defends her chickens beneath her wings; how much more shalt thou be safe beneath the wings of God, even against the devil and his angels, the powers who fly about in mid air like hawks, to carry off the weak young one?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the comparison of the hen to the very Wisdom of God is not without ground; for Christ Himself, our Lord and Saviour, speaks of Himself as likened to a hen; “how often would I have gathered thy children,” etc. That Jerusalem would not: let us be willing.…If you consider other birds, brethren, you will find many that hatch their eggs, and keep their young warm: but none that weakens herself in sympathy with her chickens, as the hen does.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We see swallows, sparrows, and storks outside their nests, without being able to decide whether they have young or no: but we know the hen to be a mother by the weakness of her voice, and the loosening of her feathers: she changes altogether from love for her chickens: she weakens herself because they are weak.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus since we were weak, the Wisdom of God made Itself weak, when the Word was made flesh, and dwelt in us, that we might hope under His wings.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “His truth shall surround thee with a shield” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are “the wings,” the same is “the shield:” since there are neither wings nor shield.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If either were literally, how could the one be the same as the other? can wings be a shield or a shield wings?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But all these expressions, indeed, are figuratively used through likenesses.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If Christ were really a Stone, He could not be a Lion; if a Lion, He could not be a Lamb: but He is called both Lion, and Lamb, and Stone, and Calf, and anything else of the sort, metaphorically, because He is neither Stone, nor Lion, nor Lamb, nor Calf, but Jesus Christ, the Saviour of all of us, for these are likenesses, not literal names.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “His truth shall be thy shield,” it is said: a shield to assure us that He will not confound those whose trust is in themselves with those who hope in God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: One is a sinner, and the other a sinner: but suppose one that presumes upon himself is a despiser, confesses not his sins, and he will say, if my sins displeased God, He would not suffer me to live.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But another dared not even raise his eyes, but beat upon his breast, saying, “God be merciful to me a sinner.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Both this was a sinner, and that: but the one mocked, the other mourned: the one was a despiser, the other a confessor, of his sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the truth of God, which respects not persons, discerns the penitent from him who denies his sin, the humble from the proud, him who presumes upon himself from him who presumes on God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou shalt not be afraid for any terror by night.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Nor for the arrow that flieth by day, for the matter that walketh in darkness, nor for the ruin and the devil that is in the noonday” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These two clauses above correspond to the two below; “Thou shalt not fear” for “the terror by night, from the arrow that flieth by day:” both because of “the terror by night,” from “the matter that walketh in darkness:” and because of “the arrow that flieth by day,” from “the ruin of the devil of the noon-day.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What ought to be feared by night, and what by day?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When any man sins in ignorance, he sins, as it were, by night: when he sins in full knowledge, by day.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The two former sins then are the lighter: the second are much heavier; but this is obscure, and will repay your attention, if, by God’s blessing, I can explain it so that you may understand it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He calls the light temptation, which the ignorant yield to, “terror by night:” the light temptation, which assails men who well know, “the arrow that flieth by day.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are light temptations?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those which do not press upon us so urgently, as to overcome us, but may pass by quickly if declined.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Suppose these, again, heavy ones.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If the persecutor threatens, and frightens the ignorant grievously, I mean those whose faith is as yet unstable, and know not that they are Christians that they may hope for a life to come; as soon as they are alarmed with temporal ills, they imagine that Christ has forsaken them, and that they are Christians to no purpose; they are not aware that they are Christians for this reason, that they may conquer the present, and hope for the future: the matter that walketh in darkness has found and seized them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But some there are who know that they are called to a future hope; that what God has promised is not of this life, or this earth; that all these temptations must be endured, that we may receive what God hath promised us for evermore; all this they know: when however the persecutor urges them more strenuously, and plies them with threats, penalties, tortures, at length they yield, and although they are well aware of their sin, yet they fall as it were by day.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But why does he say, “at noon-day”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The persecution is very hot; and thus the noon signifies the excessive heat.…The demon that is “in the noon-day,” represents the heat of a furious persecution: for these are our Lord’s words, “The sun was up; and because they had no root, they withered away:” and when explaining it, He applies it to those who are offended when persecution ariseth, “Because they have not root in themselves.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We are therefore right in understanding by the demon that destroyeth in the noon-day, a violent persecution.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Listen, beloved, while I describe the persecution, from which the Lord hath rescued His Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At first, when the emperors and kings of the world imagined that they could extirpate from the earth the Christian name by persecution, they proclaimed, that any one who confessed himself a Christian, should be smitten.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who did not choose to be smitten, denied that he was a Christian, knowing the sin he was committing: the arrow that flieth by day reached him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But whoever regarded not the present life, but had a sure trust in a future one, avoided the arrow, by confessing himself a Christian; smitten in the flesh, he was liberated in the spirit: resting with God, he began peacefully to await the redemption of his body in the resurrection of the dead: he escaped from that temptation, from the arrow that flieth by day.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Whoever professes himself a Christian, let him be beheaded;” was as the arrow that flieth by day.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The “devil that is in the noon-day” was not yet abroad, burning with a terrible persecution, and afflicting with great heat even the strong.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For hear what followed; when the enemy saw that many were hastening to martyrdom, and that the number of fresh converts increased in proportion to that of the sufferers, they said among themselves, We shall annihilate the human race, so many thousands are there who believe in His Name; if we kill all of them, there will hardly be a survivor on earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The sun then began to blaze, and to glow with a terrible heat.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Their first edict had been, Whoever shall confess himself a Christian, let him be smitten.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Their second edict was, Whoever shall have confessed himself a Christian, let him be tortured, and tortured even until he deny himself a Christian.…Many therefore who denied not, failed amid the tortures; for they were tortured until they denied.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But to those who persevered in professing Christ, what could the sword do, by killing the body at one stroke, and sending the soul to God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This was the result of protracted tortures also: yet who could be found able to resist such cruel and continued torments?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Many failed: those, I believe, who presumed upon themselves, who dwelt not under the defence of the Most High, and under the shadow of the God of Heaven; who said not to the Lord, “Thou art my lifter up:” who trusted not beneath the shadow of His wings, but reposed much confidence in their own strength.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They are thrown down by God, to show them that it is He that protects them, He overrules their temptations, He allows so much only to befall them, as each person can sustain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Many then fell before the demon of the noon-day.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Would ye know how many?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He goes on, and says, “A thousand shall fall beside thee, and ten thousand at thy right hand; but it shall not come nigh thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To whom, brethren, but to Christ Jesus, is this said?…For the members, the body, and the head, are not separate from one another: the body and the head are the Church and her Saviour.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How then is it said, “A thousand shall fall beside thee, and ten thousand by thy right hand”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because they shall fall before the devil, that destroyeth at noon.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is a terrible thing, my brethren, to fall from beside Christ, from His right hand but how shall they fall from beside Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why the one beside Him, the other at His right hand?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why a thousand beside Him, ten thousand at His right hand?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why a thousand beside Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because a thousand are fewer than the ten thousand who shall fall at His right hand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who these are will soon be clear in Christ’s name; for to some He promised that they should judge with Him, namely, to the Apostles, who left all things, and followed Him.…Those judges then are the heads of the Church, the perfect.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To such He said, “If thou wilt be perfect, go and sell that thou hast, and give to the poor.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What means the expression, “if thou wilt be perfect”? it means, if thou wilt judge with Me, and not be judged.…Many such at that period, who had distributed their all to the poor, and already promised themselves a seat beside Christ in judgment of the nations, failed amid their torments under the blazing fire of persecution, as before the demon of the noon-day, and denied Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are they who have fallen “beside” Him: when about to sit with Christ for the judgment of the world, they fell.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will now explain who are they who fall on the right hand of Christ.…And because many have fallen from that hope of being judges, but yet many, many more from that of being on His right hand, the Psalmist thus addresses Christ, “A thousand shall fall beside Thee, and ten thousand at Thy right hand.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And since there shall be many, who regarded not all these things, with whom, as it were with His own limbs, Christ is one, he adds, “But it shall not come nigh Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Were these words addressed to the Head alone?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Surely not; surely neither (doth it come nigh) to Paul, nor Peter, nor all the Apostles, nor all the Martyrs, who failed not in their torments.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then do the words, “it shall not come nigh,” mean?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why were they thus tortured?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The torture came nigh the flesh, but it did not reach the region of faith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Their faith then was far beyond the reach of the terrors threatened by their torturers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them torture, terror will not come nigh; let them torture, but they will mock the torture, putting their trust in Him who conquered before them, that the rest might conquer.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And who conquer, except they who trust not in themselves?…Who will not fear?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who trusts not in himself, but in Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But those who trust in themselves, although they even hope to judge at the side of Christ, although they hoped they should be at His right hand, as if He said to them, “Come, ye blessed of My Father,” etc.; yet the devil that is at noon overtook them, the raging heat of persecution, terrifying with violence; and many fell from the hope of the seat of judgment, of whom it is said, “A thousand shall fall beside thee;” many too fell from the hope of reward for their duties, of whom it was said, “And ten thousand at thy right hand.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But this downfall and devil that is at noon-day “shall not come nigh thee,” that is, the Head and the body; for the Lord knows who are His.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Nevertheless, with thine eyes shalt thou behold, and see the reward of the ungodly” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why “nevertheless”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because the wicked were allowed to tyrannize over Thy servants, and to persecute them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Will they then have been allowed to persecute Thy servants with impunity?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not with impunity, for although Thou hast permitted them, and Thine own have thence received a brighter crown, “nevertheless,” etc. For the evil which they willed, not the good they unconsciously were the agents of, will be recompensed them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All that is wanting is the eye of faith, by which we may see that they are raised for a time only, while they shall mourn for evermore; and to those into whose hands is given temporal power over the servants of God, it shall be said, “Depart into everlasting fire, prepared for the devil and his angels.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if every man have but eyes in the sense in which it is said, “With thine eyes shalt thou behold,” it is no unimportant thing to look upon the wicked flourishing in this life, and to have an eye to him, to consider what will become of him in the end, if he fail to reform his ways: for those who now would thunder upon others, will afterwards feel the thunderbolt themselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thou, Lord, art my hope” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He has now come to the power Which rescues him from falling by the “downfall and the devil of the noon-day.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thou, Lord, art my hope: Thou hast set Thy house of defence very high.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What do the words “very high” mean?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For many make their house of defence in God a mere refuge from temporal persecution; but the defence of God is on high, and very secret, whither thou mayest fly from the wrath to come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Within “Thou hast set thine house of defence very high.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There shall no evil happen unto Thee: neither shall any plague come nigh Thy dwelling” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Holy City is not the Church of this country only, but of the whole world as well: not that of this age only, but from Abel himself down to those who shall to the end be born and believe in Christ, the whole assembly of the Saints, belonging to one city; which city is Christ’s body, of which Christ is the Head.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There, too, dwell the Angels, who are our fellow-citizens: we toil, because we are as yet pilgrims: while they within that city are awaiting our arrival.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Letters have reached us too from that city, apart from which we are wandering: those letters are the Scriptures, which exhort us to live well.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why do I speak of letters only?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The King himself descended, and became a path to us in our wanderings: that walking in Him, we may neither stray, nor faint nor fall among robbers, nor be caught in the snares that are set near our path.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This character, then, we recognise in the whole Person of Christ, together with the Church.…He Himself is our Head, He is God, co-equal with the Father, the Word of God, by whom all things were made: but God to create, Man to renew; God to make, Man to restore.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Looking upon Him, then, let us hear the Psalm.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Listen, beloved.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the teaching and doctrine of this school, which may enable you to understand, not this Psalm only, but many, if ye keep in mind this rule.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sometimes a Psalm, and all prophecy as well, in speaking of Christ, praises the Head alone, and sometimes from the Head goes to the Body, that is, the Church, and without apparently changing the Person spoken of: because the Head is not separate from the Body, and both are spoken of as one…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then, my brethren, what is said of our Head?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thou, Lord, art my hope,” etc. Of this we have spoken, “for He hath given His angels charge over Thee, to keep Thee in all Thy ways” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: You heard these words but now, when the Gospel was being read; attend therefore.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our Lord, after He was baptized, fasted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why was He baptized?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That we might not scorn to be baptized.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when John said to our Lord, “Comest Thou to me to be baptized?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I ought to be baptized by Thee;” and our Lord replied, “Suffer it to be so now, for thus it becometh us to fulfil all righteousness;” He wished to fulfil all humility, so that He should be washed, who had no defilement.…Our Lord, then, was baptized, and after baptism He was tempted; He fasted forty days, a number which has, as I have often mentioned, a deep meaning.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All things cannot be explained at once, lest needful time be too much taken up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: After forty days He was an hungred.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He could have fasted without ever feeling hunger; but then how could He be tempted? or had He not overcome the tempter, how couldest thou learn to struggle with him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was hungry; and then the tempter said, “If Thou be the Son of God, command that these stones be made bread.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Was it a great thing for our Lord Jesus Christ to make bread out of stones, when He satisfied so many thousands with five loaves?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He made bread out of nothing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For whence came that quantity of food, which could satisfy so many thousands?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The sources of that bread are in the Lord’s hands.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is nothing wonderful; for He Himself made out of five loaves bread enough for so many thousands, who also every day out of a few seeds raises up on earth immense harvests.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are the miracles of our Lord: but from their constant operation they are disregarded.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then, my brethren, was it impossible for the Lord to create bread out of stones?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He made men even out of stones, in the words of John the Baptist himself, “God is able of these stones to raise up children unto Abraham.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why then did He not so?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That he might teach thee how to answer the tempter, so that if thou wast reduced to any straits and the tempter suggested, if thou wast a Christian and belongedst to Christ, would He desert thee now?…Listen to our Lord: “Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceedeth out of the mouth of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou think the word of God bread?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If the Word of God, through which all things were made, was not bread, He would not say, “I am the bread which came down from heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast therefore learnt to answer the tempter, when pressed with hunger.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What if he tempt thee in these words: If thou wast a Christian, thou wouldest do miracles, as many Christians have done?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou, deceived by a wicked suggestion, wouldest tempt the Lord thy God, so as to say to Him, If I am a Christian, and am before Thine eyes, and Thou dost account me at all in the number of Thine own, let me also do something like the many works which Thy Saints have done.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast tempted God, as if thou wert not a Christian, unless thou didst this.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Many who desired such things have fallen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that Simon the sorcerer desired such gifts of the Apostles, when he wished to buy the Holy Spirit for money.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He loved the power of working miracles, but loved not the imitation of humility.…What then, if he tempt thee thus, “work miracles”? that thou mayest not tempt God, what shouldest thou answer?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What our Lord answered.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The devil said to Him, “Cast Thyself down; for it is written, He shall give His Angels charge concerning Thee,” etc. If Thou shalt cast Thyself down, Angels shall receive Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And it might indeed, my brethren, happen, if our Lord had cast Himself down, the attending Angels would receive our Lord’s flesh; but what does He say to him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “It is written again, Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou thinkest Me a man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the devil came to Him with this view, that he might try whether He were the Son of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saw His Flesh; but His might appeared in His works: the Angels had borne witness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saw that He was mortal, so that he might tempt Him, that by Christ’s temptation the Christian might be taught.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then is written?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us not then tempt the Lord, so as to say, If we belong to Thee, let us work a miracle.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us return to the words of the Psalm.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They shall bear Thee in their hands, lest at any time Thou hurt Thy foot against a stone” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ was raised up in the hands of Angels, when He was taken up into heaven: not that, if Angels had not sustained Him, He would have fallen: but because they were attending on their King.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Say not, Those who sustained Him are better than He who was sustained.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Are then cattle better than men, because they sustain the weakness of men?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And we ought not to speak thus either; for if the cattle withdraw their support, their riders fall.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But how ought we to speak of it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is said even of God, “Heaven is My throne.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because then heaven supports Him, and God sits thereon, is therefore heaven the better?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus also in this Psalm we may understand it of the service of the Angels: it does not pertain to any infirmity in our Lord, but to the honour they pay, and to their service.…What the finger of God is, the Gospel explaineth to us; for the finger of God is the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How do we prove this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our Lord, when answering those who accused Him of casting out devils in the name of Beelzebub, saith, “If I cast out devils by the Spirit of God;” and another Evangelist, in relating the same saying, saith, “If I with the finger of God cast out devils.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What therefore is in one stated clearly, is darkly expressed in another.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou didst not know what was the finger of God, but another Evangelist explains it by terming it the Spirit of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Law then written by the finger of God was given on the fiftieth day after the slaughter of the lamb, and the Holy Ghost descended on the fiftieth day after the Passion of our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lamb was slain, the Passover was celebrated, the fifty days were completed, and the Law was given.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But that Law was to cause fear, not love: but that fear might be changed into love, He who was truly righteous was slain: of whom that lamb whom the Jews were slaying was the type.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He arose from the dead: and from the day of our Lord’s Passover, as from that of the slaying of the Paschal lamb, fifty days are counted; and the Holy Ghost descended, now in the fulness of love, not in the punishment of fear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why have I said this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this then our Lord arose, and was glorified, that He might send His Holy Spirit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And I said long ago that this was so, because His head is in heaven, His feet on earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If His head is in heaven, His feet on earth; what means our Lord’s feet on earth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our Lord’s saints on earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are our Lord’s feet?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Apostles sent throughout the whole world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are our Lord’s feet?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All the Evangelists, in whom our Lord travelleth over all nations.…We need not therefore wonder that our Lord was raised up to heaven by the hands of Angels, that His foot might not dash against a stone: lest those who on earth toiled in His body, while they were travelling over the whole world might become guilty of the Law, He took from them fear, and filled them with love.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Through fear Peter thrice denied Him, for he had not yet received the Holy Ghost: afterwards, when he had received the Holy Spirit, he began to preach with confidence.…Our Lord so dealt with him, as if He said, thrice thou hast denied Me through fear: thrice confess Me through love.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With that love and that charity He filled His disciples.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because He hath set His house of defence very high: because when glorified He sent the Holy Ghost, He released the faithful from the guilt of the Law, that His feet might not dash against a stone.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou shalt go upon the asp and the basilisk; the lion and the dragon shalt thou tread under thy feet” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye know who the serpent is, and how the Church treadeth upon him, as she is not conquered, because she is on her guard against his cunning.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And after what manner he is a lion and a dragon, I believe you know also, beloved.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The lion openly rages, the dragon lies secretly in covert: the devil hath each of these forces and powers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When the Martyrs were being slain, it was the raging lion: when heretics are plotting, it is the dragon creeping beneath us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast conquered the lion; conquer also the dragon: the lion hath not crushed thee, let not the dragon deceive thee.…A few women in the Church have bodily virginity: but the virginity of the heart all the faithful have.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the very matter of faith he feared that the heart’s virginity would be corrupted by the devil: and those who have lost it, are uselessly virgins in their bodies.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What does a woman who is corrupt in heart preserve in her body?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus a Catholic married woman is before a virgin heretic.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the first is not indeed a virgin in her body, but the second has become married in her heart; and married not unto God as her husband, but unto the dragon.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what shall the Church do?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The basilisk is the king of serpents, as the devil is the king of wicked spirits.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are the words of God to the Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Because he hath set his love in me, therefore will I deliver him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not only therefore the Head, which now sits in heaven, because He hath set His house of defence very high, to which no evil shall happen, neither shall any plague come nigh His dwelling; but we also who are toiling on earth, and are still living in temptations, whose steps are feared for, lest they fall into snares, may hear the voice of the Lord our God consoling us, and saying to us, “Because he hath set his love upon me, therefore will I deliver him: I will set him up, because he hath known my name.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He shall call upon me, and I will hear him: yea, I am with him in trouble” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Fear not when thou art in trouble, as if the Lord were not with thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let faith be with thee, and God is with thee in thy trouble.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are waves on the sea, and thou art tossed in thy bark, because Christ sleepeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ slept in the ship, while the men were perishing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thy faith sleep in thy heart, Christ is as it were sleeping in thy ship: because Christ dwelleth in thee through faith, when thou beginnest to be tossed, awake Christ sleeping: rouse up thy faith, and thou shalt be assured that He deserts thee not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But thou thinkest thou art forsaken, because He rescueth thee not when thou thyself dost wish.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He delivered the Three Children from the fire?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did He, who did this, desert the Maccabees?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He delivered both of these: the first bodily, that the faithless might be confounded; the last spiritually, that the faithful might imitate them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will deliver him, and bring him to honour.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With length of days will I satisfy him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is length of days?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Eternal life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Brethren, imagine not that length of days is spoken of in the same sense as days are said to be long in summer, short in winter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hath he such days to give us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That length is one that hath no end, eternal life, that is promised us in long days.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And truly, since this sufficeth, with reason he saith, “will I satisfy him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is long in time, if it hath an end, satisfieth us not: for that reason it should not be even called long.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And if we are covetous, we ought to be covetous of eternal life: long for such a life, as hath no end.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lo, a line in which our covetousness may be extended.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou wish money without limit?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Long for eternal life without limit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou wish that thy possession may have no end?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Seek for eternal life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will show him my salvation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor is this, my brethren, to be briefly passed over.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will show him my salvation:” He means, I will show him Christ Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Was He not seen on earth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What great thing hath He to show us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But He did not appear such as we shall see Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He appeared in that shape in which those who saw Him crucified Him: behold, those who saw Him, crucified Him: we have not seen Him, yet we have believed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They had eyes, have not we? yea, we too have the eyes of the heart: but, as yet we see through faith, not by sight.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When will it be sight?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When shall we, as the Apostle saith, see Him “face to face”? which God promiseth us as the high reward of all our toils.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whatever thou toilest in, thou toilest for this purpose, that thou mayest see Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Some great thing it is we are to see, since all our reward is seeing; and our Lord Jesus Christ is that very great sight.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who appeared humble, will Himself appear great, and will rejoice us, as He is even now seen of His Angels.…Let us love and imitate Him: let us run after his ointments, as is said in the Song of Solomon: “Because of the savour of thy good ointments, we will run after thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He came, and gave forth a savour that filled the world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence was that fragrance?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Follow then towards heaven, if thou do not answer falsely when it is said, “Lift up your hearts,” lift up your thoughts, your love, your hope: that it may not rot upon the earth.…“For wherever thy treasure is, there will be thy heart also.”
